title: App Home Proof
prompt:
Create one polished business visual review image for the prompt family: App Home Proof.
Digital product anchor: judgemebro.com.
Product UI and workflow facts to preserve: Fast Gen Z competitive AI dilemma game. Preserve dark judgemebro.com UI, lime get judged get points energy, 1v1 battle faceoff, AI judge verdict, rating points, friend challenge links, streamer room with viewer answers, and clean mobile-first social challenge feel. Avoid roast-app cruelty, corporate SaaS blandness, fake endorsements, fake pricing claims, over-dense UI text, and board-game metaphors.
The visual must be clearly about this digital product and should not introduce another digital product, app, SaaS workflow, or UI category.
Family intent: A hero-style placement where the app home screen proves the main value.
Business goal: Show what the product is and why the user should believe the offer.
Scene: A clean product hero composition with one large app screen, sparse supporting context, and a clear workflow state..
Subject slot: slotType: digital product UI; placement: large central screen; scale: primary proof object; interaction: ready-to-use workflow state; preserve: product surface, core workflow, approved proof cues.
Digital product proof: make the product UI, workflow state, and interface feedback the visual evidence.
UI fidelity: use sparse, plausible labels and placeholder data only; do not invent real customer names, private data, or unsupported metrics.
Do not drift into generic abstract SaaS imagery when UI/product proof is requested.
Focus: Product UI as proof, not decoration.
Decision being supported: Choose whether the app screen can carry the first impression.
Include: large product UI, workflow state, supporting proof cue, clean background.
Constraints: make the UI the visual evidence; use sparse placeholder labels; preserve supplied product facts; keep text sparse; use short labels, UI placeholder blocks, or visual markers only when needed; keep the product UI, workflow, and product-proof cues recognizable.
Avoid: generic laptop mockup, abstract gradient dashboard, invented metrics, fake customer logos; fake endorsements; unsupported claims; misspelled large text; watermarks; clutter; other brands.
